sections.paragraph:
ματαιόω (s. μάταιος) pres. 3 pl. ματαιοῦσιν Jer 23:16. Pass.: fut. 3 sg. ματαιωθήσεται; 1 aor. ἐματαιώθην; pf. μεματαίωμαι (Herodian, Gramm. I 453, 13; schol. on Soph., Trach. 258 Papag.; Dositheus 71, 17; otherw. in bibl. and eccl. usage; LXX) render futile/worthless pass. be given over to worthlessness, think about idle, worthless things, be foolish (1 Ch 21:8) ἐματαιώθησαν ἐν τοῖς διαλογισμοῖς αὐτῶν their thoughts became directed to worthless things Ro 1:21 (w. ref. to idolatry; s. μάταιος and cp. Jer 2:5 ἐπορεύθησαν ὀπίσω τῶν ματαίων καὶ ἐματαιώθησαν).—DELG s.v. μάτη.

pres. pres. = present

  1. pl. = plural

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Dositheus Dositheus , gramm., noted for a bilingual work: Ars Grammatica, IV? a.d.—List 5

otherw. otherw. = otherwise

bibl. bibl. = biblical

eccl. eccl. = ecclesiastical

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ref. ref. = reference(s)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 621
